title: Keeping Unmatched Rows with LEFT JOIN

theory.concept:
LEFT JOIN returns every row from the left table, plus matching rows from the right table — if there's no match, the right table's columns come back as NULL.

- Every row from the left table appears at least once, matched or not
- To find left-table rows with no match, filter with WHERE right_table.column IS NULL
- The join condition works the same as INNER JOIN — only the treatment of unmatched left rows differs
theory.syntax: SELECT columns FROM table1 LEFT JOIN table2 ON table1.column = table2.column;

shared_table.name: Students
shared_table.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Students (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT,
  age INTEGER,
  city TEXT,
  marks INTEGER
);
shared_table.insert_sql:
INSERT INTO Students (id, name, age, city, marks) VALUES
(1, 'Aditya', 21, 'Kolkata', 78),
(2, 'Priya', 22, 'Delhi', 85),
(3, 'Rohan', 21, 'Kolkata', 62),
(4, 'Sneha', 23, 'Kolkata', 91),
(5, 'Karan', 21, 'Bangalore', 55),
(6, 'Anjali', 22, 'Delhi', 73),
(7, 'Vikram', 20, 'Chennai', 88),
(8, 'Meera', 22, 'Delhi', 67);

shared_table.name: Enrollments
shared_table.insert_sql:
INSERT INTO Enrollments (id, student_id, course) VALUES
(1, 1, 'DBMS'),
(2, 1, 'Networks'),
(3, 2, 'DBMS'),
(4, 4, 'OS'),
(5, 5, 'DBMS'),
(6, 7, 'Networks'),
(7, 9, 'DBMS');

questions.prompt: List every student's name and course, including students with no enrollment.
Find the names of students who are not enrolled in any course.
List the name and course of students from Kolkata or Bangalore, including those without an enrollment.
